pr.title: Address Stack and Arena pointers in ASM
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Replaced hard-coded stack and arena memory addresses in assembly with linker/symbol names across MetroTRK, Dolphin SDK, init, and db assembly. The PR did not decompile new C code, but improved ASM readability and relocatable symbol intent by naming addresses such as _db_stack_addr, _db_stack_end, _stack_end, and __ArenaHi.
what_changed: In InitMetroTRK, replaced the literal 0x804F0C00 stack address load with _db_stack_addr@h/_db_stack_addr@l.

In asm/dolphin/dolphin_sdk_1.s, replaced several fallback stack/arena constants with symbols: 0x804F0C00 became _db_stack_addr, 0x804EEC00 became _db_stack_end, 0x81700000 became __ArenaHi, and 0x804DEC00 became _stack_end.
In __OSThreadInit, changed thread stack boundary setup from raw constants to _db_stack_end and _stack_end.
In asm/init.s func_80005340, initialized r1 from _db_stack_end instead of hard-coded 0x804EEC00.
In asm/melee/db/code_80225374.s func_802256CC, changed stack-reporting arithmetic and OSReport arguments to use _stack_end and _db_stack_end symbols instead of raw address literals.
smart_moves: Kept instruction encodings and immediate split form intact while replacing only the assembler expressions, preserving matching behavior.
Used high/low relocation suffixes matching the original instruction patterns: @h/@l for lis+ori and @ha/@l for lis+addi.
Converted repeated literals to shared semantic symbols, making future C decompilation and stack-related analysis easier to follow.
Applied the same symbol names consistently across unrelated assembly regions that referenced the same stack boundaries.
decomp_lessons: When an address literal corresponds to a known linker symbol or memory-region boundary, prefer the symbolic name in ASM to document intent without affecting generated code.
For matching ASM cleanup, preserve the original instruction form and relocation style; only change operands from numeric constants to equivalent symbols.
Stack setup and debug stack checks often reference the same boundary addresses from multiple subsystems, so symbolizing one occurrence should prompt a repository-wide search for the same literal.
Semantic labels such as _db_stack_addr, _db_stack_end, _stack_end, and __ArenaHi are useful anchors for later structuring startup, OS thread, arena, and debug code.
naming_conventions: Underscore-prefixed linker-style symbols were used for stack boundaries: _db_stack_addr, _db_stack_end, and _stack_end.
The arena high symbol appears as __ArenaHi, matching Dolphin/OS-style double-underscore global naming.
Existing unknown function and label names such as func_802256CC, func_803444E0, lbl_803430A0, and lbl_803EA784 were not renamed in this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace numeric address operands in lis/addi or lis/ori pairs with symbol@ha/symbol@l or symbol@h/symbol@l while preserving comments, opcodes, and register usage.
For addresses loaded with addi after lis, use @ha on the high half when that is what the original expression used.
For addresses loaded with ori after lis, use @h on the high half, as seen in InitMetroTRK and func_80005340.
This tactic improves symbolic readability while keeping the assembled machine code equivalent, which is useful for non-decompiled ASM files.
searchable_terms: Address Stack and Arena pointers in ASM
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining raw stack boundary literals 0x804F0C00, 0x804EEC00, and 0x804DEC00 in ASM and C.
Search for remaining raw arena high literal 0x81700000 and replace with __ArenaHi where equivalent.
Audit startup and OS thread initialization code for other linker-boundary constants that should be symbolized.
